summary: Exit-family archetype with computable filter and adequate sample base; structurally distinct from existing entry-heavy registry and well-targeted at the trail-family near-zero-max gap.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Exit-family archetype targeting trail kernel with widened 2.0% step parameter — addresses pattern_miner finding that trail family max ≈ -0.006 (parameter mis-tune, not dead concept). Existing registry has 101 exits but no swept wider-trail variant per R2 synthesizer's self-identified X101+ exit-side starvation gap. The liquidity_bucket=high filter further differentiates by restricting to deeper books where wider trails are more justifiable.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Exit archetype applies across all entry-matched triggers; with liquidity_bucket=high filter ≈ ~30% of universe. Across top entries (3000-5000 trig each) × 0.30 high-liquidity rate ≈ 900-1500 candidate trigger contexts; conservatively 300+ after entry-pairing constraints.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Explicitly targets the trail exit family. Note: trail step (2.0%) is an exit-kernel parameter, not a filter condition — it will be passed as exit-config to the GPU sweep, not parsed by bwe_loop_entry_filter. Proposal is well-formed: 1/1 supported filter conditions, exit param sits in the correct config slot. Strongly recommend pairing with high-lift long-side entries (E126, E064, E051) where wider trail can capture extended pump legs without premature stop-out.
